title: ophrys sphegodes subsp. atrata
intro: Ophrys sphegodes subsp. atrata, also known as the bee orchid, is a species of orchid that belongs to the Ophrys genus, characterized by its unique appearance and fragrance that mimics the scent of female bees to attract pollinators. Traditionally, the bee orchid has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Bee orchid can be prepared into herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal agent.
